description:
You are acting as a storage administrator who needs to identify where Python’s package manager (pip) stores its cache on this machine so you can decide whether to clear space later.  
    1. Create the directory “/home/user/disk_maintenance” if it does not already exist.  
    2. Using exactly one terminal command, discover the absolute path to pip’s cache directory and write **only that path** to a new file called  
       /home/user/disk_maintenance/pip_cache_path.txt  
       – The command must overwrite any existing file of the same name (truncate if it exists).  
       – The command must not append anything else (no headers, no extra lines, no trailing spaces, no newline characters other than the single newline automatically emitted by the shell redirection).  
    3. Expected format of /home/user/disk_maintenance/pip_cache_path.txt (strict):  
       • It must contain exactly one line.  
       • That single line must be the absolute path reported by pip (e.g. “/home/user/.cache/pip”).  
       • No leading or trailing whitespace is allowed.  
       • Outside of that one newline, the file must have no additional blank lines or content.  

    Once this file is created, you are done.  The automated checker will:  
       a. Confirm /home/user/disk_maintenance exists.  
       b. Confirm /home/user/disk_maintenance/pip_cache_path.txt exists.  
       c. Confirm the file has exactly one non-blank line that matches the output of the command “pip cache dir”.
truth:
Expected file after successful completion:
    Path: /home/user/disk_maintenance/pip_cache_path.txt  
    Content (single line, terminating with a newline):
    /home/user/.cache/pip

    The directory /home/user/disk_maintenance does not exist before the task begins; the agent must create it.  
    No other files are needed.
